machine.name: localai/rag_corrective_cycle
machine.description: Corrective RAG routing machine. Reads retrieval + grading signals from the Perception Engine and asserts one of three routing decisions: generate, rewrite, or abort. Replaces the keyword-overlap heuristic in grade_documents() with observable CES logic.

normalization.kept_ratio: kept_count / max(retrieved_count, 1)  — 1.0 = all docs passed grading
normalization.rewrite_count_norm: min(rewrite_count / 2, 1.0)  — 2 rewrites maps to 1.0, triggers abort

routingLogic.mutualExclusivity: the three guards partition the state space; exactly one fires per step

metadata.description: Fires [1,0,0,0] when enough documents were retrieved AND the grader kept the majority. The doc_count guard (threshold 0.1) prevents firing on empty retrieval; the kept_ratio guard (threshold 0.5) ensures most retrieved docs survived grading.
generate — graded documents are sufficient for answer generation

metadata.name: GENERATE_CHECK
metadata.description: element[0]: doc_count_norm HIGH (>= 0.1, GTE split at 0.1); element[4]: kept_ratio HIGH (>= 0.5, GTE split at 0.5); all others: wildcard (Threshold ±0.5 spans full [0,1] range)
Fires [0,0,1,0] when the grader rejected most docs AND we have already rewritten the query at least once. Signals the graph to generate with whatever context exists rather than looping further.
abort — max rewrites exhausted with poor grading; fall through to generate

metadata.name: ABORT_CHECK
metadata.description: element[4]: kept_ratio LOW (< 0.5, GTE split at 0.5 with value=0); element[5]: rewrite_count_norm HIGH (>= 0.5, GTE split at 0.5); all others: wildcard
Fires [0,1,0,0] when the grader rejected most docs and we have not yet rewritten the query. The query should be rephrased to improve retrieval on the next cycle.
rewrite — poor grading on first attempt; rephrase the question

metadata.name: REWRITE_CHECK
metadata.description: element[4]: kept_ratio LOW (< 0.5); element[5]: rewrite_count_norm LOW (< 0.5); all others: wildcard. Mutually exclusive with abort-path because rewrite_count cannot be both HIGH and LOW.

inputSequences.name: Happy Path — good retrieval, no rewrites
inputSequences.description: 5 docs retrieved (norm=0.5), all 5 kept (ratio=1.0), first attempt (rewrite_norm=0). Should assert generate [1,0,0,0].

metadata.notes: doc_count_norm=0.5 (≥0.1 HIGH), kept_ratio=1.0 (≥0.5 HIGH) → generate fires

inputSequences.name: Rewrite Path — poor grading, first attempt
inputSequences.description: 3 docs retrieved (norm=0.3) but none passed keyword grading (ratio=0.0), first attempt (rewrite_norm=0). Should assert rewrite [0,1,0,0].

metadata.notes: kept_ratio=0.0 (<0.5 LOW), rewrite_norm=0.0 (<0.5 LOW) → rewrite fires

inputSequences.name: Abort Path — poor grading, already rewritten
inputSequences.description: 2 docs retrieved (norm=0.2), none kept (ratio=0.0), already rewritten once (rewrite_norm=0.5). Should assert abort [0,0,1,0].

metadata.notes: kept_ratio=0.0 (<0.5 LOW), rewrite_norm=0.5 (≥0.5 HIGH) → abort fires

inputSequences.name: Generate After Rewrite — docs improved post-rephrase
inputSequences.description: After one rewrite the revised query found 4 docs (norm=0.4) with 80% passing grading (ratio=0.8). Rewrite_norm=0.5 (1 rewrite done). Should still assert generate — good docs win over rewrite count.

metadata.notes: doc_count=0.4 (≥0.1 HIGH), kept_ratio=0.8 (≥0.5 HIGH) → generate fires even though 1 rewrite was done
